# Transcrição: Cold_Reading_Statements

- **Fonte:** Cold_Reading_Statements
- **Duração:** 01:50
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

---

## [00:00 - 01:50]

People often ask me, Vin, when I meet somebody new, what do I say? I don't know what to say. Well, here's a cool technique for you. You can use a technique called cold reading statements. When you meet somebody for the first time, pay attention to how they appear and how they come across and pay attention to how they communicate and then make a positive statement about one of their traits. So for example, if I see someone dress in a really funky way or talk in a really enthusiastic manner, I could make a statement that is in the form of a question like this. Hey, just having met you for 30 seconds, you seem like someone who's extremely adventurous. The conversation at this point can go in one of three directions. Direction number one, you are completely wrong. And if you're completely wrong, that's okay. You gave them a lovely compliment. And if you are wrong, they're going to correct you and say to you, actually, I'm not that adventurous. And I would like to be more adventurous, which means you've just learned something new about them. Direction number two, you could be completely wrong still, but then they're going to ask you, what made you think that? I'm not an adventurous person, but why did you say that? And this allows you to have an opportunity to share them the nice compliment. Oh, the way you speak, you speak so freely. And I saw a bunch of stickers on your luggage. And I just thought you've may have visited all those places. And that just makes you come across observant and it makes you seem really present. Or the third direction is you are correct. You are correct. And people love it when this happens. When you call out a positive trait for someone and it actually matches who they are, they feel like you know them. Every single outcome from this technique is positive. You have nothing to lose. Just make sure it's positive. Obviously, don't do things like this. Hey, you look like someone who's really smelling. Hey, you look like someone who has no friends. Hey, you look like someone who's trying desperately to be funny at the end of a video that's gone for way too long.
